name: libclass-c3-perl
title: libclass-c3-perl
summary: pragma for using the C3 method resolution order
description:
pragma for using the C3 method resolution order
Class::C3 is a Perl pragma that modifies the standard method resolution order
from depth-first left-to-right to the more sophisticated C3 method resolution
order.
.
The C3 algorithm aims to provide a sane method resolution order with multiple
inheritance. It was first introduced in the Dylan language, later adopted for
Python 2.3. It is also the canonical MRO implementation for Perl 6 classes
and is the default for Parrot objects as well.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libclass-c3-perl because pragma for using the C3 method resolution order.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libclass-c3-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libclass-c3-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libclass-c3-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
